methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Mujin is a Series C robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised $85M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine same-sector companies at similar or adjacent funding stages, comprising one public company and eight private firms. Key private comparables include 1X Technologies, Exotec, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, and Hadrian, spanning valuations from $300M to $10.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach, anchoring on total capital raised as a proxy, applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-later-stage progression, yielding $144M. The peer-median method examines nine robotics and manufacturing firms at comparable stages; the median valuation across this set is $1.6B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B. This reflects material heterogeneity in the peer set, driven by differences in commercialization maturity and market capture.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a range of $123M to $1.8B, with a median of $872M. The wide spread reflects the substantial variance within the robotics sector at this stage. The stage-progression method yields a conservative floor given the limited public trading comparables and the nascent nature of several high-valuation peers. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, path to meaningful revenue scale, and timing of next funding round, all of which will materially narrow this range upon deeper diligence.
